title: Proclamation No. 24, s. 1936
text:
PROCLAMATION NO. 24

RESERVING FOR RAILROAD PURPOSES FIVE PARCELS OF LAND IN THE CITY OF MANILA, ISLAND OF LUZON

Upon the recommendation of the Secretary of Agriculture and Commerce and pursuant to the provisions of section eighty-one of Act Numbered Twenty-eight hundred and seventy-four, as amended, I hereby withdraw from sale or settlement and reserve for railroad purposes, under the administration of the Manila Railroad Company, subject to private rights, if any there be, the following-described parcels of the public domain situated in the City of Manila, Island of Luzon, and particularly described in Bureau of Lands plan Bsd—4867, to wit:

Lot No. 1-B, The Manila Railroad Co .—A parcel of land (lot No. 1-B of the subdivision plan Bsd-4867, being a portion of lot No. 1 of plan SH-154), situated in the District of San Nicolas, City of Manila. Bounded on the E., by lot No. 4, block No. 2701, lot No. 5, block No. 1820, and lot No. 2-C of the subdivision plan; on the S., by lots Nos. 2-C, 1-A, and 2-D of the subdivision plan; and on the NW. by Manila Bay and lot No. 2-B of plan Psu-46494-Amd. Beginning at a point marked “1” on plan Psu-46494-Amd. Beginning at a point marked “1” on plan, being S. 47ْْ 34’ W., 348.59 m., more or less, from B. L. L. M. No. 15, Manila cadastre, and N.21ْ 54’ W., 618.03 m., more or less, from B. L. L. M. No. 27, Manila cadastre: thence S. 0ْ 05’ E., 282.86 m. to point “2;” thence S. 16ْ 18’ W., 71.94 m. to point “3;” thence S. 89ْ 54’ W., 60.14 m. to point “4;” thence N. 0ْ 03’ w., 48.75 m. to point “5;’ thence S. 88ْ 53’ W., 156.00 m. to point “6”; thence S. 44ْ 59’ W., 65.16 m. to point “7”; thence W. 89ْ 54’ W., 86.51 m. to point “8”; thence N. 44ْ 59’ E., 519.29 m. to point “9”; thence S. 1ْ 39’ E., 14.77 m. to the point of beginning; containing an area of fifty-eight thousand four hundred thirty-eight square meters and twenty square decimeters (58,438.20), more or less. All points referred to are indicated on the plan; and on the ground, points “1,” “2,” “3,” “8,” and “9,” are marked by old B. L. concrete monuments, 15 by 60 cm.; and points “4,” “5,” and “7,” by B. L. concrete monuments, 15 by 60 cm.; bearings true; declination, variable; date of the original survey, September, 1914-January 1915, and May 3-5, 1923, and that of the subdivision survey, October 8 and 12, 1935.

Lot No. 2-C, The Manila Railroad Co .—A parcel of land (lot No. 2-C of the subdivision plan Bsd-4867, being a portion of lot No. 2 of plan SH-154, G. L. R. O. Cadastral Record No. 90), situated in the District of San Nicolas, City of Manila. Bounded on the N., by lot 1-B of the subdivision plan; on the NE., by lot No. 5, block No. 1820; on the E., by Calle del Pan, lots Nos. 1, 18, 17, and 16, block No. 1813, lot No. 4, block No. 1809, Calle Vives, lots Nos. 9, 1, 8, 7, 1, 6, and 5, block No. 1805, Calle San Nicolas, lots Nos 2 and 1, block No. 1802, and Muelle de la Industria; on the S., by Muelle de la Industria; and on the W., by lots Nos. 2-B, 2-A, and 1-B of the subdivision plan. Beginning at a point marked “1” on plan, being S. 24ْْ 09’ W., 595.03 m., more or less, from B. L. L. M. No. 15 and N. 39ْ 12’ W., 342.90 m., more or less form B. L. L. M. No. 27, Manila cadastre; thence N. 53ْ 45’ W.,  11.89 m. to point “2;” thence N. 53ْ 45’ W., 4.74 m. to point “3;” thence S. 10 ْ36’ W., 63.62 m. to point “4;” thence S. 38 ْ08’ W., 16.09 m. to point “5;” thence N. 75 ْ19’ W., 0.19 m. to point “6;” thence S. 38 ْ 31’ W., 14.89 m. to point “7;” thence S. 25 ْ 28’ E., 0.31 m. to point “8;” thence S. 38 ْ08’ W., 48.71 m. to point “9;” thence S. 38 ْ08’ W., 2.66 m. to point “10;” thence S. 61 ْ17’ W., 7.84 m. to point “11;” thence S. 60 ْ20’ W., 5.29 m. to point “12;” thence S. 28 ْ23’ E., 23.70 m. to point “13;” thence S. 0 ْ50’ E., 11.11 m. to point “14;” thence S. 49 ْ21’ W., 4.73m. to point “15;” thence S. 28 ْ56’ E., 7.68 m. to point “16;” thence S. 0 ْ50’ E., 74.73 m. to point “17;” thence S. 88 ْ18’ W., 0.88 m. to point “18;” thence S. 4 ْ03’ E., 1.41 m. to point “19;” thence N. 86 ْ28’ E., 0.80 m. to point “20;” thence S. 0 ْ49’ E., 9.11 m. to point “21;” thence S. 0 ْ50’ E., 24.36 m. to point “22;” thence S. 0 ْ50’ E., 7.16 m. to point “23;” thence N. 89 10’ W., 19.25 m. to point “24;” thence N. 0 03’ W., 7.18m. to point “26;” thence N. 89 ْ54’ E., 60.14 m. to point “27;” thence N. 16 ْ18’ E., 71.94 m. to point “28;” thence S. 42 ْ44’ E., 20.42 m. to point “29;” thence S. 2 ْ29’ W., 9.84 m. to the point of beginning; containing an area of six thousand seven hundred ninety-six square meters and eighty square decimeters (6,796.80), more or less. All points referred to are indicated on the plan; and on the ground, points “1” and “2” are marked by old P. L. S./B.L. concrete monuments 15 by 15 by 60 cm.; points “3,” “9,” “10,” “13,” “14,” “16,” “17,” “20,” “22,” “23,” “27,” “28,” and “29,” by old B. L. concrete monuments 15 by 60 cm.; point “4,” by H.L. monument No. 2; points “5,” and “6,” by old spikes corner of wall; points “7,” “8,” and “15,” by small galvanized iron spikes; points “11” and “12,” by small galvanized iron spikes in wooden posts; points “18” and “19,” by galvanized iron spikes in wall; point “24,” by cross on granite street flooring; and points “25” and “26,” by B. L. concrete monuments 15 by 60 cm.; bearings true; declination, variable; date of the original survey, September, 1914-January, 1915, and May 3-5, 1923, and that of the subdivision survey, October 8-12, 1935.

Lot No. 2-D, The Manila Railroad Co .—A parcel of land (lot No. 2-D of the subdivision plan Bsd-4867, being a portion of lot No. 2 of plan SH-154, G. L. R. O. Cadastral Record No. 90), situated in the District of San Nicolas, City of Manila. Bounded on the N., by Manila Bay and lot No. 1-B of the subdivision plan; on the SE, by lot No. 2-A of the subdivision plan; on the S., by lots Nos. 3 and 2, block No. 1801 and Manila Bay; and on the W., by Manila Bay Beginning at a point marked “1” on plan, being S. 42 ْ29’ W., 796.80 m., more or less, from B. L. L. M. No. 15 Manila cadastre, and N. 66 ْ38’ W., 557.30 m., more or less, from B. L. L. M. No. 27, Manila cadastre: thence S. 44 ْ59’ W., 49.50 m. to point “2;” thence N. 88 ْ48’ W., 46.71 m. to point “3;” thence N. 88 ْ48’ W., 77.64 m. to point “4;” thence N. 88 ْ48’ W., 77.64 m. to point “4;”  thence N. 88 48’ W., 6. 37 m. to point “5;” thence North 20.00 m. to point “6;” thence East 67.04 m. to point “7;” thence N. 44 ْ59’ E., 17.18 m. to point “8;” thence N. 89 ْ54’ E., 86.51 m. to the point of beginning; containing an area of four thousand one hundred six square meters (4,106), more or less. All points referred to are indicated on the plan; and on the ground, points, “1” and “2” are marked by B. L. concrete monuments 15 by 60 cm.; points “3,” “5,” “6,” “7,” and “8,” by old B. L. concrete monuments 15 by 60 cm.; and point “4,” by bottom of retaining sea wall; bearings true; declination, variable; date of the original survey, September, 1914-January, 1915, and May 3-5, 1923, and that of the subdivision survey, October 8 and 12, 1935.

Lot No. 4, The Manila Railroad Co .—A parcel of land (lot No. 4 of plan SH-154, being identical with lot No. 5, block No. 1820, of the cadastral survey of the City of Manila, G. L. R. O. Cadastral Record No. 90), situated in the District of San Nicolas, City of Manila. Bounded on the east, block No. 2701, lots Nos. 4, 2, 3, of block No. 1820, and Calle del Pan; on the southwest, by lot No. 2-C of plan Bsd-4867; on the west, by lot No. 1-B of plan Bsd-4867; and on the northwest, by lot No. 5. Beginning at a point marked “1” on plan, being N. 83 ْ47’ W., 85.67 m., more or less, from fire hydrant No. 318, City of Manila; thence S. 5ْ 52’ E., 26.80 m. to point “2:” thence S. 17 ْ16’ W., 22.87 m. to point “3;” thence S. 7 ْ15’ W., 28.89 m. to point “4;” thence S. 22 ْ28’ W., 8.38 m. to point “5;” thence S. 38 ْ56’ W., 15.00 m. to point “6;” thence S. 15 ْ04 W., 13.55 m. to point “7;” thence S. 5 ْ10’ E., 11.88 m. to point “8;” thence S. 23 ْ26’ E., 8.10 m. to point “9;” thence S. 1 ْ26’ E., 45.42 m. to point “10;” thence S. 9 ْ45’ W., 34.86 m. to point “11;” thence S. 2 ْ29’ W., 5.83 m. to point “12;” thence N. 42 ْ44’ W., 20.42 m. to point “13;” thence N. 0 ْ05’ W., 282.86 m. to point “14;” thence N. 43 ْ52’ E., 53.36 m. to point “15;” thence S.

1 ْ57’ E., 25.46 m. to point “16;” thence S. 31 ْ09’ W., 8.62 m. to point “17;” thence S. 14 ْ59’ W., 18.91 m. to point “18;” thence S. 8 ْ33’ W., 10.29 m. to point “19;” thence S. 41 ْ33’  W., 15.89 m. to point  “20;” thence S. 47 ْ48’ E., 14.79 m. to point “21;” thence S. 3 ْ49’ E., 10.96 m. to point “22;” thence S. 19 ْ53’ E., 17.82 m. to point “23;” thence S. 23 ْ05’ E., 11.99 m. to the point of beginning; containing an area of eight thousand three hundred fifty-three square meters and fifty square decimeters (8,353.50), more or less. All points referred to are indicated on the plan; and on the ground, points “1,” “16,” “17,” “18,” “22,” and “23,” are marked by crosses on stones; points “2,” “3,” and “5” to “11,” inclusive, by old P. L. S. /B. L. concrete monuments; point “4,” by H. L. M. No. 3; point “12,” by intersections; points “13” and “14,” by old B. L.  concrete monuments; point “15,” by old stake; and points “19,” “20,” and “21,” by galvanized iron spikes; bearings true; declination, variable; date of the cadastral survey, September, 1914, to January, 1915, and May 3-5, 1923.

Lot N. 5, The Manila Railroad Co .—A panel of land (lot No. 5 plan SH-154, being identical with lot No. 4, block No. 2701 of the cadastral survey of the City of Manila, G. L. R. O. Cadastral Record No. 90), situated in the District of San Nicolas, City of Manila. Bounded on the east, by block No. 2701; on the southeast, by lot No. 4; on the west, by lot No. 1-B of plan Bsd-4867; and on the northwest, by lot No. 1-B of plan Psu-46494-AMD. Beginning at a point marked “1” on plan, being S. 47 ْ34’ W., 348.59 m., more or less, from B. L. L. M. No. 15, Manila Cadastre, and N. 21 ْ54’ W., 618.03 m. more or less, from B. L. L. M. No. 27, Manila cadastre; thence N. 1 ْ39’ W., 14.77 m. to point “2,” thence N. 44 ْ69’ E., 51.70 m. to point “3;” thence S. 3 ْ39’ E., 12.90 m. to point “4;” thence S. 43 ْ52’ W., 53.36 m. to the point of beginning; containing an area of five hundred thirty-one square meters and forty square decimeters (531.40), more or less. All points referred to are indicated on the plan; and on the ground point “1” is marked by old B. L. concrete monument; points “2” and “3,” by intersections; and point “4,” by old stake; bearings true; declination, variable; date of the cadastral survey, September, 1914, to January, 1915 and May 3-5, 1923.

In witness whereof, I have hereunto set my hand and caused the seal of the Commonwealth of the Philippines to be affixed.

Done at the City of Manila, this seventeenth day of January, in the year of Our Lord, nineteen hundred and thirty-six, and of the Commonwealth of the Philippines, the first.

[SEAL]

President of the Philippines

By the President:

Secretary of the Interior
